diagrams.title: Data Communication System Model
diagrams.description: Shows five components: Sender → Message → Protocol → Transmission Medium → Receiver
diagrams.purpose: Understanding basic data communication architecture and components interaction

diagrams.title: Transmission Modes Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of simplex, half-duplex, and full-duplex data flow
diagrams.purpose: Distinguishing between different directions of data flow in communication systems

diagrams.title: Signal Attenuation Graph
diagrams.description: Graph showing signal strength decreasing with distance
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how signal degrades over transmission distance and need for repeaters

diagrams.title: Network Topologies
elements: Star: Central hub with radiating lines
diagrams.purpose: Understanding different network structures and their fault tolerance characteristics

diagrams.title: Guided Media Comparison
diagrams.description: Cross-section comparison of twisted pair, coaxial, and fiber optic cables
elements: Twisted Pair: Two insulated copper wires twisted
Coaxial: Central copper core with shielding layers
Fiber Optic: Glass core with cladding and jacket
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing physical construction differences and understanding why fiber outperforms electrically
